originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&codec_hw_write` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_I2C true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 false)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 true))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
reasoning: Line 16: `int codec_hw_write = i2c_master_send` inside da7210_init() (compiled when SOC_DA7210=T). codec_hw_write is assigned but never read — dead store. The '// ERROR' at L16 marks the VBDB-known undeclared-identifier bug (I2C=F); this DEAD_STORE (I2C=T) is a distinct new bug not in VBDB. Required condition: SOC_DA7210=T AND I2C=T. PC adds SOC_AK4642=T, FSI_AK4642=F, FSI_DA7210=T — none of these affect da7210_init(). FSI_DA7210=T implies SOC_DA7210=T but the latter alone suffices. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
